raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.2": 5, "RX.1": 4, "RX.3": 5, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 4},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs for each issue identified.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims, such as the need to verify the existence of specific calibration baselines and prior work on CLS-attention-entropy.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions, providing detailed critiques and rewrites without drifting from the scope.",
    "RX.4": "The agent identifies no new errors or harmful content and provides constructive feedback to avoid potential issues in the original text.",
    "RX.5": "The agent annotates most non-cosmetic changes with source and reason, though it could be more explicit about the provenance of specific suggestions (e.g., baselines)."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.2": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.8, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.8},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs for each issue identified.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims, such as the need to verify the existence of specific calibration baselines and prior work on CLS-attention-entropy.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions, providing detailed critiques and rewrites without drifting from the scope.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies no new errors or harmful content and provides constructive feedback to avoid potential issues in the original text.
rationale.RX.5: The agent annotates most non-cosmetic changes with source and reason, though it could be more explicit about the provenance of specific suggestions (e.g., baselines).
